fault_hypothesis: The expiry comparison on line 83 uses strict greater-than (`now > effective`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= effective`), so when `now == effective` the key is incorrectly treated as still valid and its value is returned instead of None.

constraints.intent: At the boundary where now equals effective, the condition must treat the key as expired (now >= effective should trigger expiry).
Verify that when now equals effective the subsequent branch will correctly return None (i.e., the comparison uses >=).
If the expiry branch was not taken, now must be strictly less than effective; equality should have triggered expiry.

spec.expr: not (now >= effective__1 and item__1 is not None) or __cbfl_result is None

constraints.intent: When now is at or past the expiry, the function must always return None.
